summary: Clear soundboard recording captures a solid 1994 show highlighted by Bird Song, Black Peter and an outstanding Iko Iko, though the set’s close on Throwing Stones draws criticism.
review: The SBD capture is generally clean and well‑balanced, letting Jerry’s “tasty” guitar work shine through especially on Black Peter and the Iko Iko.  Fans praise several jams – Bird Song, Estimated Jam and The Other One – but note a tired feel from Garcia and a disappointing Throwing Stones finale that leaves the second set feeling uneven.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the performance positively (5‑star) while a few note fatigue and weak moments; recording quality is consistently described as good to excellent, yielding an overall rating around 3.7.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear, well‑balanced SBD capture with no major dropouts; several reviewers call the recording “excellent” or “great.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as tighter and focused, second set looser with a strong early half‑step opener but a weaker close on Throwing Stones; overall song selection is solid though some feel the show lacks extra “umph.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry’s firing‑off, tasty lead work and dexterity, especially on Black Peter’s coda and the Estimated jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards receive praise for staying in check and adding pleasant texture; one reviewer calls his playing a “nice surprise.”
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers sound good, with a short drum segment noted as appropriate for the period.
